title: Downloads: Saving from the Internet
drDigitalIntro: When you download something — a PDF, an image, a document — it comes from the internet to your Mac. Click a download link and Safari saves it to your Downloads folder. You can see recent downloads in Safari's sidebar or press Command+Y to open the Downloads panel. From there, you can open the file, show it in Finder, or move it to a folder.

playgroundTask.question: Where do files go when you download them in Safari?

drDigitalSuccess: The Downloads folder! It's in Finder's sidebar. Always check there when you can't find something you downloaded.
drDigitalHint: Safari always puts downloaded files in the same place so you know where to look.
